howItWorks.title: How the Karaoke Video Maker Works

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, or OGG file — or paste a link. No account needed to start, and no manual lyric typing required. The AI works directly from your audio — you don't need to build a karaoke timing map from scratch.

steps.title: AI transcribes and syncs
steps.description: AI transcribes lyrics from the audio automatically and syncs every line to the beat — in 50+ languages. Click any line to edit the words before export. The cue timing is designed so the next lyric appears before the singer needs it.

steps.title: Preview and export
steps.description: Watch the karaoke video like a singer would — check that lyric cues arrive on time, text is readable on any screen, and choruses feel natural. Export in 16:9 for YouTube or TV screens, 9:16 for TikTok, or 1:1 for feed posts. Free to export, no subscription required.

whyChoose.title: Why Make Karaoke Videos on LyricVideoMaker
whyChoose.description: Making a karaoke video manually means transcribing lyrics, timing each line to the music, and testing readability for sing-along. LyricVideoMaker does all of that automatically — upload the song and get a sing-along lyric video.

highlight.title: AI handles the hardest part: lyrics and timing
highlight.description: Karaoke timing has to be tighter than a regular lyric video — the next line must appear before the singer needs it. AI transcription and beat sync get this right automatically.

cards.title: Cue timing for singers
cards.description: Lines appear with enough lead time for someone to sing along, instead of popping up after the vocal has already started.

cards.title: Sing-along readable
cards.description: Lyrics are timed and displayed for people who are singing, not just reading. Line-by-line cues arrive early enough to follow and stay readable on phones, TVs, and projectors.

cards.title: Consistent karaoke channel output
cards.description: Use a repeatable look across songs so a YouTube karaoke channel or event playlist feels consistent from track to track.

items.question: Is this karaoke video maker free?
items.answer: Yes. Sign up, get free credits, and export your karaoke video — no subscription required, no credit card needed.

items.question: How to make a karaoke video without typing lyrics?
items.answer: No. Upload the song and AI transcribes every lyric line automatically in 50+ languages, then times the cues for sing-along. Edit any line before export.

items.question: Is this the same as vocal removal?
items.answer: No. This karaoke video maker creates sing-along lyric videos with beat-synced text. It works with the original track — vocal removal is a separate tool.

items.question: Can I make karaoke videos for a YouTube channel?
items.answer: Yes. Use the same upload-to-export flow for every song. Export in 16:9 for consistent YouTube karaoke content.

items.question: Can I use exported karaoke videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes. The karaoke videos you export are yours for monetized channels, events, and venues. Just make sure you have the rights to the songs you upload.

items.question: What formats can I export karaoke videos in?
items.answer: 16:9 for YouTube and TV screens, 9:16 for mobile karaoke and TikTok, or 1:1 for feed posts. All formats from one upload.

contentSections.heading: What makes a good karaoke video
body: A good karaoke video lives or dies on timing. The next line has to appear before the singer needs it, the current line has to stay readable at a glance, and the highlight has to track the beat so people know exactly where they are in the song. The karaoke video maker handles all three automatically from your uploaded audio.

Because the lyrics are transcribed and beat-synced rather than dropped onto a rough timer, cues land early enough to follow and the text stays large and legible — the difference between a video people can actually sing to and one they give up on after the first chorus.

contentSections.heading: Sing-along mode and true karaoke
body: The karaoke video maker supports two ways to perform. Sing-along mode keeps the original track and overlays beat-synced, highlighted lyrics so a room can join in instantly. True karaoke mode removes the lead vocal so the singer carries the melody, with the lyric cues guiding them line by line.

Both modes start from the same upload and the same synced lyrics, so you can produce a sing-along version for casual nights and a vocal-removed version for serious practice or a karaoke channel without rebuilding the project.

contentSections.heading: Karaoke videos for parties, channels, and practice
body: For parties and events, a karaoke video maker turns any song into instant entertainment — no special hardware, just a screen and the synced lyrics. For YouTube karaoke channels, a repeatable upload-to-export flow keeps a back catalog looking consistent track after track.

For singers and students, karaoke videos are a practice tool: the cue timing trains phrasing and breath control, and being able to fix any lyric line means the words on screen are the words actually being sung.

contentSections.heading: Readable on phones, TVs, and projectors
body: Karaoke gets watched on everything from a phone propped on a table to a living-room TV to a projector at a venue. The karaoke video maker sizes and places the lyrics so they stay readable across all of them, and the beat-synced highlight keeps a whole group locked to the same line.
Export in 16:9 for TVs and YouTube, 9:16 for mobile karaoke and short-form clips, or 1:1 for feed posts — one upload covers the screen wherever the singing happens.

contentSections.heading: How to make a karaoke video without typing lyrics
body: Upload your song and the karaoke video maker transcribes every line automatically, in 50+ languages, then syncs the cues to the beat. You never start from a blank timeline or a lyrics document — the hardest part of making a karaoke video is already done before you touch anything.

From there you review the lines, adjust any word, choose a highlight style, preview how it sings, and export. What used to take an evening of manual timing in a video editor now takes a few minutes in the browser.

contentSections.heading: Karaoke video maker vs vocal-removal apps
body: A vocal-removal app only strips the lead vocal — you are left with a backing track and no words on screen. A karaoke video maker does the part that actually matters for singing: it transcribes the lyrics, times the cues to the beat, highlights each line as it comes, and renders a video you can play anywhere.

With true karaoke mode you get both at once — the lead vocal removed and synced, highlighted lyrics on screen — so there is no stitching a muted track together with a separate lyrics file. One upload becomes a complete, performable karaoke video.

contentSections.heading: From upload to a finished karaoke video
body: The full path is short. Upload your song, let the AI transcribe and beat-sync the lyrics, choose sing-along or true karaoke, pick a highlight style, and preview how it performs on screen. When the cues land where you want them, export for the screen you will play it on.

Everything runs in the browser, so there is no software to learn and no project files to manage. Because the lyrics are editable at every step, the words on screen always match the words being sung — which is exactly what separates a karaoke video people enjoy from one that throws them off.
